item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very enjoyable! Multiple dimensional SCPs are always so fun to read! 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Typo in the subway log

D-1321 run back to the

Anyways, writing is a bit choppy and dialogue is stiff, but good article nonetheless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do the doors persists ir demanifest? What if the chalk is erased?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Augh, this is so creepy. Gave me chills the whole time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-093 is my favorite of the classics. I wouldn't mind more like it. With the spoiler connection, this is almost better than SCP-093.1

My only complaint is that this seems too short for so epic a subject matter. Still probably in the top 10% of all SCPs that make it into positives.

Footnotes
1. Also, my upvote brought this to +93

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Upon entry, subject found themself in a building that resembled a Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV), devoid of human life."

That, right there, is the true definition of horror. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, here goes. Don't really think I have to collapse 'spoilers' this far down the comment chair so BE WARNED readers who…didn't read it yet I guess.

The Black chalk lead to Limbo, yes. Purgatory would be a different book (Same with Paradiso) So I left them out. The green area the 8th circle of hell, which is split up into eight Bolgias. The punishments in these include being wrapped and bitten by snakes and contracting all kinds of illnesses, but being unable to die, so they suffered.

Lastly, I don't think anything would happen if you drew a holy symbol with the chalk. they piece only work if you draw the right signal with the right color chalk. Maybe there's a version out there that deals with Purgatory and Paradiso.

EDIT: Oops missed yellow. In the original text, the sin isn't really greed. That's half the sin. It's more of the miserly and prodigal, where half the people spent all the money they had and the other half saved it all. They were forced to run into each other for all of eternity, so this one is a more straightforward reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As one who does not know Dante's Hell piece, to me this feels like SCP-093, just less good. There is nothing technically wrong with it, so Novote, bordering on downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the few skips that have made me think after reading the comments, 'WOAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH.' Great work.

+1

I too have a spoiler question…

+ What did the potato say to the steak?
- Nice to meatcha!

Is the black area limbo or purgatory? Also, I don't really understand the CDC in the green area. That one is envy, right?

EDIT: Two more things… I don't get yellow. I know it's greed, but the cars confuse me. Why that instead of something involving boiling liquids? And what would happen if somebody tried to draw things associated with heaven, like a cross or an angel symbol?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I +1 this for a brilliant atmosphere, especially in the metropolis bit. My only problem is that the interviewer was quick to question the D-class person's fear of the corpses by the wall. I'd be pretty damn creeped out if I saw that many corpses. I think it would've worked better if the D-guy's different perspective of the corpses was revealled subtley or gradually through the conversation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel I should read Commedia to understand this better…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I was on the fence about this because some test readers could figure it out before that part and some couldn't until that part. I'm going to leave it in for now unless more people suggest against it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting because: a) I'll never get tired of exploration log-based SCPs, and b) because I appreciate your bravery in pushing the grammatical envelope with your use of "themself" as a third person singular reflexive pronoun. It took me a few seconds to get over the wiggly red line I mentally projected under that word, but upon further consideration I now think it's a useful construction, and I want to see that shit in the OED a decade or two from now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think actually including the line from Dante's Inferno was a little on the nose. I got what was going on long before I read that, so you could probably omit that part. Otherwise, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see this being upvoted, well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This takes be back to the first exploration logs that I read on the site, Bravo!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright that makes sense. I can headcanon that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ spoils
- oke

If I remember correctly, the river in circle seven was actually a river of burning blood where the sinners of violence were kept and shot at by centaurs. This is what the river was supposed to be here. As for the desert, I left that out because I didn't really want this to feel like a total copy of that Canto/ give more Dante hints then needed. I like to imangine there was a desert on the outskirts of the forest and that the D-class just retreated before they could see it.

The cabin itself didnt really mean anything, it was just an area for the door to lead out to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bloody Hell! It's Hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I also have a question concerning the Orange test.

+ Shpoilers 
- Shpoliers for an old poem

I felt like the Orange test didn't really relate to its circle as well as the others clearly did. While rushing water could indicate the Phlegethon and the moving trees are clearly the sinners who committed suicide, I couldn't place any allusions to the burning desert/Third Ring, nor could I immediately associate the forest/cabin with violence. Could you explain your thoughts on this test to me?

Also I like that the demons/Satan are the stone sarcophagi. I wonder if because the D-Class percieves the corpses as sinners, they see the sarcophagi as demons too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels well thought out. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, people have been mentioning orange so I'll either move it or add something there tomorrow. While I'm at it I'll probably add the chalk colors at the beginning of the logs too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a really solid Series I feel to it, when you could sit down with an article like the Red Sea Object and just read about weird places for a while. If Pata H is correct, then there's more going on here than just that, and that's rather clever.

I would suggest mentioning at the start of each exploration log which color chalk opened the door, if only because I completely forgot after reading the chart. Also, why isn't the orange the last one in the list? You'd think the one pending testing would be the last one they test. Still, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh wow, this is really clever, it wasn't until the last bit that I got it.

+ Spoilerish Speculation 
Spoilerish Speculation

It's the Nine Circles of Hell, isn't it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems good. I upvoted, but I'm curious (I may have missed something) as to why Orange testing is pending approval, but all the others have been tested already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it for the most part. Being a fan of Exploration Logs and all. But I can't shake the feeling that something is off about the tone in the logs. Maybe try and be more clinical if possible, it felt like I was being rushed though the worlds you created. They were interesting to me and I feel they would benefit from more description, more "exploration" if you will.

Also I would change a few of the D Classes quotes. I felt some of them convey very unnecessary information. I'd like to "hear" more about what they see and are experiencing and less what they had to say in the moment.

A final critique would be the use of the word "they" when referring to the D-Class. I get what you mean but it degrades the clinical tone a little and kept making me think there was more then 1 D-class present. Maybe be more specific about the test subject in question, like denoting their age and gender at the beginning of the log so you can use a more specific pronoun that doesn't imply an additional person is present.

That aside I enjoyed it except for the few things I mention that bothered me. I'll +1 because of the potential.

item.title: Here we go
item.content:encoded:
My 2nd SCP. I'm still not sure how I feel about it but it's done. I'm gonna let it sit for a few hours before thanking people, I wanna see what the community thinks first.

EDIT: I'm feeling better about it now so here I go! Thanks to GoldN4 the other night in #thecritters and Leveritas in the forum!

EDIT 2: Added the orange test due to feedback.
